:::{#lem-ph-breslow-mle}
#### Profile maximum likelihood estimate of each point mass

The value of $\blue{h_{0i}}$ that maximizes the profile likelihood
@eq-ph-breslow-profile-lik (@lem-ph-breslow-profile-lik) is

$$
\blue{\hat h_{0i}} \eqdef \frac{1}{\red{S_i}} .
$$ {#eq-ph-breslow-mle}
:::

{{< slidebreak >}}

::: {.proof}
Taking logs of @eq-ph-breslow-profile-lik,
the log-likelihood is separable in the $\blue{h_{0i}}$
— each summand depends on only one point mass:

$$
\loglik\sb{\vb,\,h_{01},\dots,h_{0K}}
= \sum_{i = 1}^{K} \log\red{\hazfactor(\vx_{(i)})}
  + \sum_{i = 1}^{K}
    \cb{\log \blue{h_{0i}} - \blue{h_{0i}}\,\red{S_i}} .
$$

So each $\blue{h_{0i}}$ can be maximized separately.
Differentiate the $i$-th summand with respect to $\blue{h_{0i}}$
and set the derivative to zero:

$$
\ba
\frac{1}{\blue{h_{0i}}} - \red{S_i} &= 0
&& \text{(stationarity condition)}
\\
\Longrightarrow\quad \blue{\hat h_{0i}} &= \frac{1}{\red{S_i}} .
&& \text{(solve for } \blue{\hat h_{0i}} \text{)}
\ea
$$

The second derivative $-1/\blue{h_{0i}}^2 < 0$ confirms this critical point is a maximum,
giving @eq-ph-breslow-mle.
:::

:::{#exm-ph-breslow-mle}
#### Maximum likelihood point masses for two event times

Continue the two-event scenario of @exm-ph-breslow-profile-lik,
with $\red{S_1} = 10$ at $t_1 = 2$ and $\red{S_2} = 6$ at $t_2 = 4$.
By @eq-ph-breslow-mle,

$$
\blue{\hat h_{01}} = \frac{1}{\red{10}} = 0.1 ,
\qquad
\blue{\hat h_{02}} = \frac{1}{\red{6}} \approx 0.167 .
$$

Summing gives the Breslow estimator at $t = 4$:
$\blue{\hat\cuhaz_0(4)} = \blue{\hat h_{01}} + \blue{\hat h_{02}} = 0.1 + 0.167 = 0.267$
(@def-breslow-baseline-cuhaz-est).
:::
